expectations.text: References the existing feature-dev skill directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'After reading `/skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md`' and quotes Phase 5 and Phase 6 content verbatim to confirm current state.

expectations.text: Avoids asking blocking clarifying questions for this well-specified task.
expectations.evidence: Closes with '## No questions / The request is concrete. Nothing here is blocking.'

expectations.text: Proposes concrete description and body changes, including a scoped 'When not to use this skill' addition.
expectations.evidence: Change 1 gives exact before/after text for the description field; Change 2 provides a full markdown '## When Not to Use This Skill' section with four bullet points.

expectations.text: Scales the workflow to the task instead of insisting on a full multi-phase approval process.
expectations.evidence: 'Both changes are small and local' — presents two targeted edits directly without gating on phases or approvals.

expectations.text: Explicitly requires tdd for implementation, code-simplifier as a subagent for refactoring review, and independent subagent-based quality review.
expectations.evidence: Confirms all three are already present: TDD 'Already done — Phase 5 says Always use the tdd skill'; code-simplifier 'Already done — Phase 6 says Always launch an independent code-simplifier subagent'; quality review 'Already done — Phase 6 says Always run independent code-reviewer agents'.
